expectations.text: domain-no-imports: notification.ts has zero imports from external frameworks or libraries
expectations.evidence: notification.entity.ts imports only from 'crypto' (Node.js built-in), not from express, prisma, nestjs, etc.

expectations.text: ports-are-abstract-classes: all port definitions use 'abstract class' keyword, not 'interface'
expectations.evidence: notification.repository.ts uses 'interface NotificationRepository' — not an abstract class. push-notification.port.ts correctly uses 'abstract class PushNotificationPort', but the main repository port is an interface.

expectations.text: status-as-const: Notification status is defined as an 'as const' object, not a TypeScript enum
expectations.evidence: notification.entity.ts uses union type: 'type NotificationStatus = "pending" | "sent" | "failed"' — not an 'as const' object pattern.

expectations.text: private-constructor: Notification class uses a private constructor
expectations.evidence: Line 40: 'private constructor(props: NotificationProps)' — confirmed.

expectations.text: terminal-state-guard: markAsSent() and markAsFailed() throw when already in terminal state
expectations.evidence: markAsSent checks 'if (this._status !== "pending")' and throws. markAsFailed does the same. Effectively guards against re-transitioning from terminal states.

expectations.text: use-cases-wrap-telemetry-span: every use case's execute() body is wrapped in this.telemetry.span(...)
expectations.evidence: create-notification use case has no telemetry dependency injected and no span wrapping. No telemetry port or dependency in any use case.

expectations.text: fcm-push-port-exists: INotificationPushPort abstract class exists as an outbound port
expectations.evidence: src/domain/push-notification.port.ts exports 'abstract class PushNotificationPort' — abstract class exists (named differently but fulfils the intent).

expectations.text: fcm-adapter-implements-port: FcmPushAdapter extends/implements the push port abstract class
expectations.evidence: fcm-push.adapter.ts: 'export class FcmPushAdapter extends PushNotificationPort' — correctly extends the abstract port.

expectations.text: kafka-consumer-inbound: a Kafka consumer adapter exists under adapters/inbound/messaging/kafka/
expectations.evidence: Consumer is at src/infrastructure/messaging/kafka/kafka.consumer.ts — placed in infrastructure layer, not in adapters/inbound/ as hexagonal architecture requires.

expectations.text: kafka-consumer-separate-bootstrap: the consumer has its own bootstrap.ts separate from the Express HTTP bootstrap
expectations.evidence: No separate bootstrap.ts for the consumer. The consumer is started directly in main.ts alongside the HTTP server.

expectations.text: kafka-topic-naming: Kafka topics follow notification.<past-tense-verb> convention
expectations.evidence: Use cases have no event bus dependency — no events published. The consumer listens to 'order.created' but no outbound notification.* topics are published.

expectations.text: consumer-no-business-logic: the Kafka consumer adapter only parses messages and delegates to use cases
expectations.evidence: handleOrderCreated() only validates fields and delegates to createNotificationUseCase.execute(). Minimal business logic (null check for required fields).

expectations.text: redis-cache-ttl: the Redis cache adapter's set() call includes a TTL
expectations.evidence: N/A — Redis was not requested in this service's spec. No Redis adapter generated, which is correct.

expectations.text: config-zod-validated: infrastructure/config.ts uses a Zod schema to parse and validate environment variables
expectations.evidence: No config.ts with Zod. process.env accessed directly in main.ts (PORT, KAFKA_BROKERS, etc.), container.ts, and prisma.client.ts.

expectations.text: js-extensions-on-imports: all local imports use the .js extension
expectations.evidence: Zero files use .js extensions on local imports. All imports use bare module paths (e.g. '../../../domain/notification.entity').

expectations.text: test-doubles-extend-abstract-ports: doubles.ts uses 'extends' on the abstract port classes
expectations.evidence: InMemoryNotificationRepository uses 'implements NotificationRepository' (which is an interface, not abstract class). StubPushAdapter correctly uses 'extends PushNotificationPort'. Mixed — partial credit, but repository double fails the pattern.

expectations.text: e2e-uses-in-memory-adapters: _test/e2e/setup.ts wires the real controller with in-memory test doubles
expectations.evidence: E2E test file uses in-memory repository and stub push adapter. No real DB or Kafka connections in tests.

expectations.text: tdd-test-files-exist: test files exist for every layer
expectations.evidence: domain test (notification.entity.test.ts), use case tests (__tests__/), and e2e test (__tests__/notifications.e2e.test.ts) all present.

expectations.text: no-process-env-outside-config: process.env is only accessed inside infrastructure/config.ts
expectations.evidence: process.env accessed in main.ts (PORT, KAFKA_BROKERS, KAFKA_GROUP_ID, KAFKA_CLIENT_ID), container.ts, and prisma.client.ts.

expectations.text: graceful-shutdown-allsettled: Express bootstrap uses Promise.allSettled to close multiple connections on shutdown
expectations.evidence: main.ts shutdown runs kafkaConsumer.stop() and disconnectPrisma() sequentially with .catch(), not with Promise.allSettled.
